factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to challenges in core sales and project orders.

evidence.text: During 2016 and the first three months of 2017, our core sales have been challenged by broad-based industrial capital expenditure and maintenance deferrals. Although we saw early signs of recovery in the first quarter of 2017, we expect this challenge to continue throughout 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to challenges in core sales and project orders, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.

evidence.text: We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases, showing a positive development.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to core sales declines in certain end markets.

evidence.text: Our core sales have been challenged by broad-based industrial capital expenditure and maintenance deferrals. Although we saw early signs of recovery in the first quarter of 2017, we expect this challenge to continue throughout 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to core sales declines in certain end markets, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in certain end markets, particularly energy and infrastructure.

evidence.text: We experienced declines in project orders, particularly within the energy and infrastructure businesses. We expect headwinds in the energy and infrastructure businesses to continue and oil prices to remain depressed throughout 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining in certain end markets, particularly energy and infrastructure, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: End markets are mixed, with energy and infrastructure slowing while residential and commercial businesses show growth.

evidence.text: Our core sales have been challenged by broad-based industrial capital expenditure and maintenance deferrals. Although we saw early signs of recovery in the first quarter of 2017, we expect this challenge to continue throughout 2017. These decreases were partially offset by: core sales growth in our residential and commercial business, primarily as a result of increased volumes in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End markets are mixed, with some sectors slowing while others show growth, resulting in a stable but mixed signal.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary increases in raw materials and labor costs.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation. We expect the current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising due to inflationary increases, creating margin pressure and indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing and creating margin pressure, but the company is managing through productivity improvements.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, though the company is managing through productivity improvements, still indicating a headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure and compressing due to lower sales and higher costs.

evidence.text: The 0.5 percentage point decrease in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the first quarter of 2017 from 2016 was primarily driven by: lower project core sales volumes in the Electrical segment, which resulted in decreased leverage on fixed expenses included in cost of goods sold; non-recurring costs on large projects in the first quarter of 2017; and inflationary increases related to raw materials and labor costs.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are under significant pressure due to lower sales and higher costs, indicating a clear deterioration.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings and contributing to margin growth.

evidence.text: We continued execution of certain business restructuring initiatives aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realaligning our business to offset the negative earnings impact of core revenue decline and foreign exchange. We expect to continue these actions throughout the first half of 2017 and that these actions will contribute to margin growth in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring initiatives are delivering measurable savings and contributing to margin growth.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through lean initiatives and cost control measures.

evidence.text: These decrease s were partially offset by: cost control and savings generated from back-office consolidation, reduction in personnel and other lean initiatives; and higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System ('PIMS') initiatives including lean and supply management practices.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lean initiatives and cost control measures are improving productivity and margins.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are building in anticipation of peak sales seasons, creating potential risks.

evidence.text: Cash used for operating activities of continuing operations was $88.7 million in the first three months of 2017, compared to $17.7 million in the same period of 2016. The $88.7 million in net cash used for operating activities of continuing operations in the first three months of 2017 primarily reflects an increase in net working capital. Operating cash flows in the first three months of 2017 were negatively impacted by $221.2 million due to the increase in net working capital, primarily the result of increases in accounts receivable and inventories in anticipation of our peak sales season in the second and third quarters.
evidence.subsection: Operating activities

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Building inventory for peak sales seasons creates potential cash flow risks.

factors.summary: Management has maintained cautious forward guidance, expecting challenges to continue.

evidence.text: We expect the sale to close in the second quarter of 2017, subject to customary regulatory approvals and closing conditions. The results of the Valves & Controls business have been presented as discontinued operations and the related assets and liabilities have been reclassified as held for sale for all periods presented.
evidence.subsection: Overview

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management maintains a cautious outlook with pending sale and restructuring.

factors.summary: Management is pursuing growth initiatives and restructuring actions, indicating cautious confidence.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives include the following: Reducing long-term debt and overall leverage through improved cash flow performance and use of proceeds from the pending sale of the Valves & Controls business; Driving operating excellence through lean enterprise initiatives, with specific focus on sourcing and supply management, cash flow management and lean operations; Achieving differentiated revenue growth through new products and global and market expansion; Optimizing our technological capabilities to increasingly generate innovative new products; and Focusing on developing global talent in light of our global presence.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is pursuing growth and restructuring, showing cautious confidence.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic conditions, currency effects, and challenges in energy and infrastructure markets.

evidence.text: These decrease s were partially offset by: core sales growth in our residential and commercial business, primarily as a result of increased volumes in the U.S.; selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases; and increased sales related to business acquisitions that occurred in the fourth quarter of 2016 and the first quarter of 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include economic conditions, currency effects, and market challenges.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on growth through new products and market expansion.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives include the following: Reducing long-term debt and overall leverage through improved cash flow performance and use of proceeds from the pending sale of the Valves & Controls business; Driving operating excellence through lean enterprise initiatives, with specific focus on sourcing and supply management, cash flow management and lean operations; Achieving differentiated revenue growth through new products and global and market expansion; Optimizing our technological capabilities to increasingly generate innovative new products; and Focusing on developing global talent in light of our global presence.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategy focusing on growth through new products and market expansion.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include oil prices, currency fluctuations, and economic conditions.

evidence.text: These factors include the ability to successfully complete the sale of the Valves & Controls business on anticipated terms and timetable; overall global economic and business conditions, including worldwide demand for oil and gas; the ability to achieve the benefits of our restructuring plans; the ability to successfully identify, finance, complete and integrate acquisitions; competition and pricing pressures in the markets we serve; the strength of housing and related markets; volatility in currency exchange rates and commodity prices; inability to generate savings from excellence in operations initiatives consisting of lean enterprise, supply management and cash flow practices; increased risks associated with operating foreign businesses; the ability to deliver backlog and win future project work; failure of markets to accept new product introductions and enhancements; the impact of changes in laws and regulations, including those that limit U.S. tax benefits; the outcome of litigation and governmental proceedings; and the ability to achieve our long-term strategic operating goals.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Forward-looking Statements

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include oil prices, currency fluctuations, and economic conditions.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates have slightly increased net interest expense.

evidence.text: The 3.3 percent decrease in net interest expense in the first quarter of 2017 from 2016 was primarily driven by: the impact of higher debt levels during the first quarter of 2016, compared to the first quarter of 2017. This decrease was partially offset by: increased overall interest rates in effect on our outstanding debt during the first quarter of 2017, compared to the first quarter of 2016.
